verdict.summary: Guide is useful but has several material legal inaccuracies: it uses wrong dangerous-dog licence categories, overstates/understates some obligations, gives a likely wrong SIAC URL, and misstates rabies, abandonment penalties and containment rules.

issues.current: Опасные породы (Categoria A/B по DL 315/2009)
issues.problem: Dangerous / potentially dangerous dogs are not municipal licence categories A/B. In Portugal dog licence categories are different; dangerous/potentially dangerous dogs are normally category H and G respectively. DL 315/2009 uses the legal concepts “cão perigoso” and “cão potencialmente perigoso”, not “Categoria A/B”. This error is repeated throughout the guide.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: “Опасные и потенциально опасные собаки по DL 315/2009: licenças especiais na Junta, обычно categorias H (perigoso) и G (potencialmente perigoso), со страховкой RC минимум €50 000, курсом владельца и особыми правилами выгула.”

issues.current: ⚠️ Опасные породы — Categoria A и B
issues.problem: Wrong category labels. Category A/B is not the legal split for dangerous breeds in Portuguese dog licensing. This will mislead users at the Junta de Freguesia.
issues.suggestion: Change title to: “⚠️ Опасные и потенциально опасные собаки — categorias G/H”.

issues.current: Список пород (Categoria B — potencialmente perigoso)
issues.problem: Potentially dangerous breed list exists, but calling it “Categoria B” is wrong. The licence category for potentially dangerous dogs is not B.
issues.suggestion: Write: “Список пород потенциально опасных собак (cães potencialmente perigosos; licença municipal geralmente categoria G)”.

issues.current: Метисы этих пород (≥ 50% крови)
issues.problem: The law does not use a simple “≥50% blood” threshold. It covers crosses of the listed breeds and dogs with the relevant morphological/typological characteristics, depending on the legal wording and official assessment. The 50% rule is an unsafe simplification.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: “Метисы/скрещивания этих пород и собаки с типологией, подпадающей под перечень потенциально опасных пород; спорные случаи лучше проверять у veterinário municipal/Junta.”

issues.current: Проверка чипа: siac.dgav.pt по номеру
issues.problem: The public SIAC site is generally siac.vet, not siac.dgav.pt. The stated URL is likely wrong or at least not the correct public reference.
issues.suggestion: Use: “Проверка/сообщение о найденном или пропавшем животном: https://www.siac.vet/”. Keep DGAV as regulator/source, not as the SIAC search URL.

issues.current: Разведение категории A/B — запрещено (с 2009) — можно только стерилизация
issues.problem: Again uses wrong categories A/B. Also the wording is too vague: Portuguese rules concern cães perigosos/potencialmente perigosos and include strict restrictions on reproduction, sale/transfer, advertising, registration and, in some cases, sterilisation requirements. “Можно только стерилизация” is not clear legal guidance.
issues.suggestion: Rewrite as: “Разведение/продажа опасных и потенциально опасных собак регулируется DL 315/2009: для cães perigosos/potencialmente perigosos действуют запреты и специальные условия; перед вязкой/передачей проверяйте DGAV/Junta/veterinário municipal. Не используйте категории A/B.”

issues.current: Без противопоказаний по психике (certidão médica)
issues.problem: A generic medical/psychiatric certificate is not one of the standard national requirements usually listed for special licence holders under DL 315/2009. Required documents include criminal record, civil liability insurance, training certificate, term of responsibility and animal documentation. This may make readers chase a document that the Junta does not require.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: “Для специальной лицензии обычно нужны: ≥18 лет, registo criminal, seguro RC, termo de responsabilidade, comprovativo de formação do detentor, boletim sanitário/SIAC и документы о стерилизации, когда применимо. Конкретный список уточнять в Junta.”

issues.current: Проходить специальный курс «formação em comportamento canino» (по некоторым муниципалитетам)
issues.problem: This is understated. Training of the holder of dangerous/potentially dangerous dogs is a national legal requirement for the special licence, not merely something required by some municipalities.
issues.suggestion: Write: “Обязателен curso/formação для detentor de cão perigoso ou potencialmente perigoso; сертификат обычно нужен для licença especial в Junta.”

issues.current: Во дворе: забор ≥ 1.8 м, табличка «cão perigoso»
issues.problem: The legal containment standard is commonly stated as at least 2 metres for the fence/enclosure, with warning signage. 1.8 m is likely below the statutory minimum.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: “Во дворе/на участке: меры усиленной безопасности, включая ограждение не менее 2 м и предупреждающую табличку («cão perigoso»/«cão potencialmente perigoso»).”

issues.current: Нельзя на детских площадках, в общ. транспорте, ресторанах
issues.problem: This is overbroad and mixes different regimes. Dangerous/potentially dangerous dogs have strict muzzle/leash and control rules in public spaces, but a blanket national ban on public transport and restaurants is not stated this way. Transport operators and restaurants may have their own rules; restaurants generally regulate animals separately from dangerous-dog law.
issues.suggestion: Rewrite: “В общественных местах — только с коротким поводком и функциональным намордником; некоторые места/операторы транспорта/рестораны могут запрещать вход по своим правилам. Детские площадки и зоны с местным запретом — не использовать.”

issues.current: Бешенство — обязательно ежегодно (подтверждается veterinário)
issues.problem: Rabies vaccination is mandatory for dogs, but “annually” is too absolute. Revaccination depends on the vaccine validity / official veterinary rules recorded in the boletim/passport; many vaccines are valid for more than one year.
issues.suggestion: Write: “Бешенство — обязательно для собак; ревакцинация по сроку действия вакцины, указанному ветеринаром в boletim/passaporte, а не всегда строго раз в год.”

issues.current: Ветеринар также проверяет blood titer при въезде из не-EU
issues.problem: Rabies antibody titre test is not required for every non-EU origin. It is required for entry from certain non-listed/high-risk third countries, under EU pet travel rules, and must be done before entry with specific timing. A Portuguese vet “checking it” after arrival is not the normal compliance step.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: “При въезде из некоторых стран вне ЕС нужен rabies antibody titre test, сделанный заранее в утверждённой лаборатории и в правильные сроки; для listed third countries требования мягче. Проверяйте правила ЕС/DGAV до поездки.”
issues.source: Regulation (EU) No 576/2013; DGAV pet travel guidance

issues.current: Брошенная собака в PT — criminal если доказан умысел (до 3 лет ареста по Lei 69/2014)
issues.problem: The penalty is misstated. Abandonment of companion animals is a criminal offence in the Penal Code, but the maximum is not simply “up to 3 years” for abandonment. Three years relates to aggravated animal mistreatment outcomes, not ordinary abandonment.
issues.suggestion: Write: “Брошенное животное — crime de abandono de animais de companhia; базово наказывается штрафом или лишением свободы до 6 месяцев, а при создании опасности для жизни/здоровья животного — строже. Отдельно maus-tratos may carry higher penalties.”

issues.current: Или GNR SEPNA (заповедная полиция) 217 653 100 — для диких / potentially dangerous cases
issues.problem: The commonly published national SEPNA/SOS Ambiente e Território contact is 808 200 520. The listed Lisbon number may be a GNR contact but is not the standard national SEPNA hotline and may misdirect readers outside Lisbon.
issues.suggestion: Use: “GNR SEPNA / Linha SOS Ambiente e Território: 808 200 520; emergency/immediate danger: 112; local municipal kennel/câmara for collection.”
issues.source: GNR SEPNA / Linha SOS Ambiente e Território

issues.current: Продление ежегодно до 31 марта
issues.problem: This is not a safe national rule. Dog licence renewal practice is municipal and linked to annual licence validity / rabies documentation; many juntas do not use a universal 31 March deadline.
issues.suggestion: Write: “Продление ежегодно в сроки вашей Junta de Freguesia; обычно при продлении нужны licença anterior, boletim/passaporte с vacina antirrábica válida и SIAC.”

issues.current: SIAC ... обязательна после microchip — бесплатно у ветеринара
issues.problem: Misleading wording. The microchip and SIAC registration are done by a vet, but users normally pay the vet for implantation/registration. SIAC itself may have a small registration fee included in the vet invoice; it is not experienced by owners as “free at the vet”.
issues.suggestion: Write: “Microchip + SIAC-регистрация делаются у ветеринара; обычно всё включено в счёт примерно €20–40/€50, отдельно ‘бесплатно’ рассчитывать не стоит.”

issues.current: Автоматически регистрируется в SIAC (centralized database) — бесплатно
issues.problem: Same issue: registration is mandatory and normally submitted by the vet, but “free” is misleading for the pet owner.
issues.suggestion: Replace with: “Ветеринар вносит животное в SIAC; стоимость обычно включена в услугу чипирования/регистрации.”

issues.current: Любая собака, показавшая агрессию к человеку или животному (вне provocación) — переводится в Categoria A (perigoso) по решению veterinário municipal
issues.problem: Wrong category label again, and the legal definition is more precise than “any aggression”. A dog can be classified as dangerous after biting/attacking/injuring/killing in legally defined circumstances or by competent authority assessment; it is not simply any aggression and not “Categoria A”.
issues.suggestion: Write: “Собака может быть признана cão perigoso при нападении/укусе/серьёзном ранении или смерти человека/животного в предусмотренных законом случаях либо по решению компетентных органов; это не ‘categoria A’.”

issues.current: Licença Canina Categoria A/B
issues.problem: Wrong category label in costs table.
issues.suggestion: Change to: “Licença especial cão potencialmente perigoso/perigoso — categorias G/H”.

issues.current: Страховка for cat A/B
issues.problem: Wrong category label and awkward English/Russian mix. Insurance applies to dangerous/potentially dangerous dogs, not “cat A/B”.
issues.suggestion: Change to: “Страховка RC для cão perigoso/potencialmente perigoso”.

issues.current: DGAV — SIAC / https://www.dgav.pt/
issues.problem: This is too generic for a guide relying heavily on SIAC and microchip obligations. It should cite the official SIAC website or the SIAC legal regime directly.
issues.suggestion: Add a specific SIAC source: “SIAC — Sistema de Informação de Animais de Companhia, https://www.siac.vet/” and/or cite the SIAC legal regime created by DL 82/2019.

issues.current: Sources list lacks Portaria 422/2004 and training/insurance sources
issues.problem: For a legally sensitive dangerous-breed guide, the breed list and owner training/insurance requirements should be sourced specifically, not only via DL 315/2009.
issues.suggestion: Add official sources: Portaria 422/2004 for the potentially dangerous breed list; Portaria 317/2015 for detentor training; relevant insurance minimum source/municipal guidance for RC coverage.

missing_topics: Clarify that dangerous/potentially dangerous dog licence categories are G/H, while ordinary municipal categories A, B, C etc. mean something else.
Mention that the special licence for dangerous/potentially dangerous dogs is issued by the Junta de Freguesia and must be renewed periodically with specific documents.
Mention mandatory owner training as a national requirement and point to recognised training entities.

Mention that dogs entering Portugal from non-EU countries must comply with EU pet travel rules: microchip before rabies vaccine, valid rabies vaccine, possible titre test, health certificate/TRACES depending on origin.
Mention that rabies vaccine validity may be more than one year depending on vaccine and veterinary record.
Add the official SIAC website and the legal source for SIAC obligations.
Add specific official source for the potentially dangerous breed list: Portaria 422/2004.
Add standard emergency/reporting contacts: 112 for immediate danger and GNR SEPNA/SOS Ambiente 808 200 520.
